name: personalize
description: Parse user's PDF resume and create personalized knowledge chapters based on their research background.
trigger: When user uploads a PDF resume or requests background customization.
workflow: 1. Resume text is pre-extracted by the server and provided in the prompt context under 【简历原文】 — do NOT run pdftotext or parse the PDF yourself.
2. Identify: published papers, research directions, core algorithms used, and skill gaps.
3. Determine which evolution paths (A/B/C/D) are applicable.

4. Research online for each applicable path — fire the searches for the independent paths (A/B/C/D) IN PARALLEL (one batch of tool calls), not sequentially, to cut wall-clock time. Search recent ArXiv papers, GitHub repos, and technical blogs.

5. Persist the retrieved originals to knowledge/[NN]_personalized_research/personalized_research.sources/ — one NN_<slug>.md per source (key excerpt + URL + retrieval date + priority P0-P4) plus _manifest.md. Reference only: never register in INDEX_KB.md, never render to HTML.
6. Synthesize all research into ONE comprehensive draft HTML file in a new numbered chapter directory under knowledge/.
7. The single output should have sections organized by path (A/B/C/D), each with its research findings integrated.
8. Register the single draft in INDEX_KB.md under Pending Review.
9. Wait for user confirmation before promoting to official status.

paths.A: Academic paper deep-dive: extract user's publications, retrieve full paper, create math derivation + PyTorch implementation chapter.
paths.B: Research frontier expansion: identify user's main direction, search recent top-venue papers, create comparative survey chapter.
paths.C: Project algorithm first-principles: extract core algorithms from user's projects, create rigorous derivation chapter.
paths.D: Gap analysis: identify blind spots between user's background and target role, create remedial knowledge chapter.
